commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds provider-runtime classification, formatter copy, and regression tests to replace raw HTTP 401 invalid-token provider errors with a friendly re-authentication hint, plus a `CHANGELOG.md` entry.

PR surface: Source +62, Tests +102, Docs +1. Total +165 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main still lacks a plain HTTP 401 invalid-token formatter branch, so those messages can fall through to raw HTTP/API payload formatting. I did not run the helper in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can port the accepted fix shape to current helper files and remove the release-owned changelog edit without deciding token-refresh product behavior.

target.title: fix(agents): sanitize raw HTTP 401 provider errors in user-visible replies (#56197)
target.body:
```
### Summary

Reported by @lokamir in #56197: when an OpenClaw embedded agent on
Feishu (or any chat surface) routes through a provider whose upstream
JWT expires mid-session — the reporter's case was Z.AI/AutoGLM proxy
auth — the assistant turn returns the raw `HTTP 401: "Invalid token"`
provider phrase as the user-visible reply text. The user sees a bare
HTTP error string in their Feishu thread instead of a friendly
re-authentication hint.

The token-refresh-and-retry path is out of scope for this fix per
clawsweeper-bot's review:

> Z.AI/AutoGLM token refresh protocol and core-versus-provider
> ownership boundary need maintainer judgment before an automated
> repair is safe.

This PR implements the bot's explicit narrow recommendation:

> keep the generic embedded-run fallback as safe re-authentication
> guidance rather than raw HTTP status text when refresh cannot happen.

### Root cause

`formatAssistantErrorText` in `src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.ts`
already translates a long list of provider error shapes (context
overflow, OAuth refresh failures, schema rejections, transport
failures, billing 402s, etc.) into friendly user-visible copy. But
plain HTTP 401 with a token-expired body — the most common shape for
API-key-auth providers like Z.AI, GLM, AutoGLM — fell through every
existing classifier branch because:

- `auth_refresh` only matches explicit OAuth-refresh failure messages,
- `auth_html_403` only matches HTML 403 bodies,
- `auth_scope` is OpenAI-Codex-specific,

…so the message reached the catch-all
`isLikelyHttpErrorText(raw) → formatRawAssistantErrorForUi(raw)` path
and the raw provider phrase was returned to the chat user.

### Fix

`src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.ts`:

1. Add a new `ProviderRuntimeFailureKind` value `"auth_invalid_token"`
   for the plain HTTP 401 / "Invalid token" / "Unauthorized" case.
2. In `classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind`, map the existing
   `classifyFailoverSignal` `auth` reason to that new kind, so the
   classification reuses the same 401/403 message-matching the
   failover logic already trusts (including the existing
   `auth_permanent` and billing precedence rules at status 401/403).
3. Place the new branch AFTER `replay_invalid` and `schema` so 401s
   with structurally-meaningful bodies — for example
   `"401 input item ID does not belong to this conversation"` →
   `replay_invalid` — keep their existing copy. The existing test
   `returns a recovery hint for replay-invalid connection mismatch
   errors` pins this ordering.
4. In `formatAssistantErrorText`, add a friendly message branch for
   `auth_invalid_token`:

   "Authentication failed (provider returned HTTP 401). Your provider
    token may have expired — try the request again in a moment. If the
    failure persists, re-authenticate this provider."

### Tests

`src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts` adds:

- The exact reported payload (`HTTP 401: "Invalid token"`) — pins the
  user-visible copy and asserts the raw `Invalid token` phrase does
  NOT leak into the friendly text.
- Three real-world 401 variants (`401 Unauthorized`,
  `HTTP 401 Unauthorized: token expired`,
  `status code: 401, message: "expired token"`) — each maps to the
  same friendly copy.
- A 401-with-billing-shape counter-test — proves the new branch does
  not collapse `Key limit exceeded` 401s into the generic re-auth
  hint, preserving the existing billing/`auth_permanent` precedence
  in `classifyFailoverSignal`.

The existing `replay_invalid` test (401 + connection-mismatch body)
continues to pass after the reordering.

### Verification

Rebased onto `upstream/main` `83d7ab0d36` (HEAD `1a5dffdd59`) to pick up the `checks-fast-contracts-plugins-c` main-churn fixes martingarramon called out; the two PR commits (`45f46749a4` for #56197 + `1a5dffdd59` for the #77394 review feedback) replay cleanly with no conflicts.

Two captures against the rebased branch, both using the same `node_modules` the CI lane uses:

**1. Direct runtime invocation of `formatAssistantErrorText`.** I imported the patched helper directly through the workspace's tsx loader and fed it the exact five error strings that exercise the #56197 fix and the @thewhaleking review controls. The output below is the actual return value the embedded runner's error path receives at runtime — the reply pipeline reads this string verbatim and feeds it to the user-facing channel:

```
=== Reproducer for openclaw #56197 / PR #77394 ===

case 1 — Feishu-embedded 401 / Invalid token (the #56197 report)
  raw: "HTTP 401: \"Invalid token\""
  formatted: "Authentication failed (provider returned HTTP 401). Your provider token may have expired — try the request again in a moment. If the failure persists, re-authenticate this provider."

case 2 — Unauthorized / token expired variant
  raw: "HTTP 401 Unauthorized: token expired"
  formatted: "Authentication failed (provider returned HTTP 401). Your provider token may have expired — try the request again in a moment. If the failure persists, re-authenticate this provider."

case 3 — plain 403 must NOT be classified as 401 (#77394 review)
  raw: "HTTP 403 Forbidden: access denied"
  formatted: "HTTP 403: Forbidden: access denied"

case 4 — message-only auth, no HTTP prefix, must NOT claim 401 (#77394 review)
  raw: "auth failed"
  formatted: "auth failed"

case 5 — 401 billing must NOT collapse into the generic re-auth hint
  raw: "HTTP 401: {\"error\":{\"code\":\"auth_permanent\",\"message\":\"payment required\"}}"
  formatted: "⚠️ feishu-embedded returned a billing error — your API key has run out of credits or has an insufficient balance. Check your feishu-embedded billing dashboard and top up or switch to a different API key."
```

Cases 1 + 2 prove the original #56197 symptom is fixed (raw `HTTP 401 / "Invalid token"` → re-auth hint). Cases 3 + 4 are the @thewhaleking review controls (plain 403 and message-only auth strings are NOT promoted into the new 401 path). Case 5 is the `auth_permanent` billing precedence gate the review asked to pin.

**2. Full regression-suite run** on the same rebased branch via the workspace's installed runner — the same binary CI's `unit-fast` lane invokes. The five new cases that pin the #56197 + #77394-review contract:

```
 ✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts > formatAssistantErrorText > sanitizes raw HTTP 401 / Invalid token errors into a re-auth hint (#56197) 0ms
 ✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts > formatAssistantErrorText > sanitizes Unauthorized / token-expired variants under HTTP 401 1ms
 ✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts > formatAssistantErrorText > does not collapse 401 billing / permanent-auth errors into the generic re-auth hint 0ms
 ✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts > formatAssistantErrorText > does not claim HTTP 401 for plain 403 errors that fall through to the generic auth reason (#77394 review) 1ms
 ✓ |unit-fast| ../../src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts > formatAssistantErrorText > does not claim HTTP 401 for message-only auth errors with no HTTP status prefix (#77394 review) 1ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  68 passed (68)
   Duration  6.12s
```

(only the 5 new #56197 / #77394-review cases shown above; the other 63 cases in the same suite all pass too — full output captured in the local run, `68 passed / 0 failed`).

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on touched files → clean.

### Out of scope (follow-ups)

- Token refresh / retry on 401 for Z.AI/AutoGLM: clawsweeper-bot
  flagged this as needing maintainer judgment on
  "Z.AI/AutoGLM token refresh protocol and core-versus-provider
  ownership boundary". The right shape is plugin-owned per
  `prepareRuntimeAuth` if the provider exposes a
  source-credential-to-runtime-token exchange.
- Generic API-key refresh in core: explicitly riskier than a
  provider-owned hook per the bot's review; should not be combined
  with this user-copy fix.

Fixes #56197.
```
target.author: jeffrey701

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
